translated: We didn't really know if each other's songs would have dancing or not.
So, when I met up with the other members, I'd be like,
'Wait, are you dancing to Kanon-chan's song?'
or 'How much dancing is there?'
We had conversations like that all the time.
Then I happened to run into Yabu right after she finished Mei-chan's choreography lesson.
She muttered something like, 'It was kind of flamenco-ish...'
And I was like, 'Wait, what do you mean?' I was so confused.
I just couldn't wrap my head around the word 'flamenco'.
Like Yabu said earlier,
I pictured her standing tall at a mic stand,
with a fiery vibe. So when I heard 'flamenco'...
I mean, you don't really get many chances to see flamenco, right?
It's not something Liella would usually do.
Definitely not.
All of us doing flamenco together?
Probably not.
It must be hard when you don't even know what the 'right' way to do it is.
It looks difficult just watching it, so I think it would've been really tough to actually do.
It's incredibly difficult.
But I saw how much you were practicing.
Yeah, your moves were so sharp!
It was so cool.
That's why...
Like Miku-san said, that part where you thrust your fist forward feels so direct and so much like Mei-chan.
I love it.
Yeah, I totally get that.
It's technically a dance move, but it doesn't feel like one.
Right.
Yeah, yeah.
It feels like you're putting your whole heart into that fist.
I found myself getting really emotional during that part too.
Mm-hmm.
That happens at the end of the first chorus, right? Or...
The end of the second chorus.
And you do it in the final chorus too, so it's the very last thing.
The movement is the same every time, but for the first,
second, and final time, the feelings behind it felt different to me.
Depending on the performance, what I'm feeling in that moment changes,
so I put all those emotions into my fist.
I see, I see.
That's so good.
Thank you.
I also really love the sound of the glass shattering.
Oh!
That part is so cool.
Right?
You can hear it even better during the live show.
Oh, definitely.
It really resonated. And the flames in the background...
matched Mei-chan so well, it was awesome.
Yeah.
And since 'Fanatic' was right after that...
Oh, that's right.
Yeah.
We were getting ready, so we were watching Mei-chan from the wings.
Feeling that heat, the two of us were like, 'Alright, let's go!'
We felt like we had to keep that momentum going.
It was really, really great.
So cool.
Thank you so much.
Can I say something too? Kanon performs before Mei-chan,
and then there's a bit of time before Kanon's next turn.
So I was able to watch Mei-chan as she was about to go on stage.
From backstage, you know.
And of course, before that too,
but I'd also see Mei-chan—I mean, Yabu—right after her performance.
Every time she finished, she had this look of passionate fulfillment,
but at the same time, she always looked like she was thinking,
'What should I do next?' She always had that expression,
Yabu did.
So, how do I put it?
She had her own sense of accomplishment from giving it her all,
but she also looked like she felt she still had a long way to go.
Backstage.
And that just felt so much like Mei-chan to me.
I was almost in tears while drinking my hot water.
Just watching her.
That's so Mei-chan.
From our perspective, it was already so wonderful.
